connections.description: OIDC, SAML, SCIM, and LDAP connections for this tenant, plus a self-serve Admin Portal your own IT admin can use without a Qeet ID account.

portal.title: Self-serve Admin Portal
portal.description: Generate a link your own IT admin can follow to configure this tenant's SAML connection and/or roll its SCIM token — no Qeet ID account or console access needed. The link carries no identity of its own; whoever holds it can act until it expires or you revoke it.
portal.copied: Link generated — copy it now (it won't be shown again):
portal.emptyDescription: Generate one above to delegate SSO/SCIM setup.

ldap.description: Bridge on-prem Active Directory or generic LDAPv3 directories. Users authenticate with their directory credentials and are provisioned on first login.

list.title: Connections
list.description: Qeet ID binds with the service account, finds the user under the base DN, then re-binds as that user to verify the password.

ldap.testTitle: Bind with the service account to verify settings

create.title: New LDAP connection
create.description: Point at your directory and supply a read-only service account.
create.serverHelp: Use ldaps:// (636) for TLS, or ldap:// (389) with StartTLS below.
create.bindPasswordHelp: Stored server-side and never returned by the API.
create.startTlsHelp: Upgrade an ldap:// connection to TLS.
create.skipVerifyHelp: Accept self-signed certs (lab only — not recommended).

samlSp.description: Service-provider–initiated SAML 2.0 connections to enterprise IdPs. Assertions are validated against the IdP signing certificate; users are provisioned on first login.

list.title: Connections
list.description: One row per IdP. JIT provisioning runs on every successful assertion.

samlSp.testSsoTitle: Open the IdP login to test this connection
samlSp.metadataTitle: Download SP metadata to hand to your IdP

validate.sheetDescription: Offline preflight of this connection's configuration. Run a full Test SSO for an end-to-end check against the IdP.

create.title: New SAML connection
create.description: Paste the IdP's SSO URL, issuer and signing certificate (from its metadata).
create.statusHelp: Draft connections accept test logins but aren't advertised.

scim.description: SCIM 2.0 endpoint for automated provisioning and deprovisioning from your IdP.

token.title: Your new SCIM bearer token
token.description: Copy it now — for security it is shown once and cannot be retrieved again.

endpoint.title: Endpoint & token
endpoint.description: Paste these into Okta / Entra ID / Google when configuring the SCIM app.
endpoint.rotateHelp: Rotating immediately invalidates the previous token; update your IdP afterwards.
endpoint.generateHelp: Generate a token to enable the SCIM endpoint.

users.title: Provisioned users
users.empty: No users provisioned over SCIM yet.

disable.title: Disable SCIM provisioning?
disable.description: The current token is revoked and your IdP will stop syncing.
disable.label: Disable

tokens.description: Active OAuth / OIDC grants. Access tokens are short-lived JWTs that expire on their own; revoking a grant invalidates its refresh-token chain so it can't be renewed.

list.title: Active grants
list.count: One row per (client, user) refresh-token grant. {{count}} active.

keys.description: Long-lived secrets your apps and scripts use to call the Qeet ID API. Send as Authorization: ApiKey <raw>.

revealed.title: Your new key — copy it now
revealed.description: This is the only time we'll show {{name}}. Store it in your secret manager.

confirm.title: Revoke "{{name}}"?
confirm.description: Any service using it will lose access immediately.
confirm.label: Revoke

create.title: New API key
create.description: The full key is shown once after creation — we only store a bcrypt hash.
create.scopesHelp: Leave empty for full access (least-privilege scoping recommended).

machineIds.description: Service accounts for server-to-server calls. Authenticate via POST /v1/oauth/token with grant_type=client_credentials.

revealed.title: Client credentials for {{name}}
revealed.description: We'll never show this secret again. Use it as client_id + client_secret on the token endpoint.

confirm.title: Disable "{{name}}"?
confirm.description: Token issuance will be blocked immediately.
confirm.label: Disable

create.title: New machine identity
create.description: Creates a service principal usable via OAuth 2.0 client_credentials.

secrets.description: Encrypted vault for integration secrets — 3rd-party API keys, tokens and signing material. Values are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest and only shown via an audited reveal.

create.title: New secret
create.description: The value is encrypted immediately and can't be read back except via reveal.
create.scopeHelp: A free-text label for grouping, e.g. the integration it belongs to.

magicLinks.description: Passwordless sign-in via a one-time link emailed to the user. Links are single-use and expire after the configured lifetime.
magicLinks.title: Magic-link sign-in
magicLinks.subtitle: Allow members to sign in with a one-time email link.
magicLinks.ttlHelp: How long a link stays valid after it's sent. Shorter is safer.

passkeys.description: WebAuthn / FIDO2 credentials registered against your account. Biometric, hardware key, and synced passkeys are all stored here.

passwordless.description: Passwordless sign-in methods your members can use instead of a password.

passkeys.title: Passkeys
passkeys.description: WebAuthn / FIDO2 — phishing-resistant, the recommended default.

magicLinks.title: Magic links
magicLinks.description: A one-time sign-in link sent to the member's email.

emailOtp.title: Email OTP
emailOtp.description: A one-time passcode delivered by email.

smsOtp.title: SMS OTP
smsOtp.description: A one-time passcode delivered by text message.

trustedDevices.title: Trusted devices (adaptive MFA)
trustedDevices.description: Let members who have completed two-factor verification skip the second factor on that device for 30 days. New or unrecognized devices are always challenged. Off by default.

passkeysMgmt.title: Passkeys management
passkeysMgmt.description: Individual passkeys are registered and revoked per device under Login methods → Passkeys.

totp.description: Time-based One-Time Password (RFC 6238). Use any TOTP authenticator: 1Password, Authy, Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, etc.

idle.title: TOTP authenticator
idle.subtitle: Not enrolled yet on this account.
idle.bullet1: • Six-digit codes generated locally on your device every 30 seconds.
idle.bullet2: • You'll get 10 single-use recovery codes after enrollment.
idle.bullet3: • HMAC-SHA1, 30-second time step, ±1 step clock-drift tolerance.

enrolling.title: Scan or paste into your authenticator
enrolling.subtitle: Add this entry, then enter the 6-digit code your app shows to confirm.

confirmed.title: TOTP enrolled
confirmed.subtitle: You'll be asked for a code on every future sign-in.
confirmed.recoveryDescription: Each code is single-use. We'll never show them again — store them in your password manager.

smsEmail.description: One-time passcodes delivered to a verified email address or phone number, used as a second factor.

list.title: OTP factors
list.subtitle: Each delivers a single-use code at sign-in. Codes expire after 10 minutes.
list.empty: No email or SMS factors yet.

smsEmail.sendTestTitle: Send a test code to verify delivery

sheet.title: Add an OTP factor
sheet.describeConfirm: Enter the code we just sent to confirm you control this destination.
sheet.describeStart: We'll send a one-time code to confirm the destination.
sheet.destinationEmailHelp: A code will be emailed here.

recoveryCodes.description: Single-use backup codes for signing in when your authenticator device is unavailable.

notEnrolled.title: Enable two-factor authentication first
notEnrolled.description: Recovery codes back up an authenticator app. Set up TOTP, then generate codes here.

fresh.title: Your new recovery codes
fresh.description: Save these now — for security they are shown once and cannot be retrieved again. Each code works a single time.

social.description: Configure OAuth client credentials for social IdPs. The sign-in flow (start / callback / token exchange) runs through the hosted login app.
social.oauth2OnlyDesc: {{label}} doesn't publish an OIDC discovery document — sign-in requires a discovery URL, so this provider can't work until a plain-OAuth-2.0 adapter ships.
social.requiresDiscovery: Requires your own OIDC discovery URL.
social.emptyTitle: No providers configured yet. Pick a provider above to enable social sign-in.

configure.title: Configure {{label}}
configure.description: Paste the OAuth client credentials from your IdP's developer console.
configure.clientSecretHelp: Stored server-side as plaintext today — rotation is your responsibility.

password.description: Password sign-in and the complexity rules enforced when members set or change a password.
password.authDescription: Allow members to sign in with an email and password.
password.complexityDescription: Enforced when a member sets or changes their password.
